finding: The root manifest currently describes Batch 94 instead of the website/PWA application.
risk: PWA metadata and application identity are invalid or absent.
action: Replace root manifest.json with one authoritative web app manifest. Keep batch manifests under versioned subfolders.

finding: The service worker deletes every cache during activation and does not cache application assets.
risk: Offline mode is non-functional and each activation wipes prior caches.
action: Create a versioned cache, precache verified shell assets, add runtime caching, offline fallback, and controlled cleanup.

finding: The sitemap contains only six top-level URLs.
risk: Temple, sloka, festival, literature, regional, and knowledge pages are not discoverable through the sitemap.
action: Generate the sitemap from verified published HTML routes only. Exclude framework, review-only, and placeholder pages.

finding: Temple, sloka, and festival detail content is injected client-side using query parameters.
risk: Deep content has weak crawlability, weak sharing metadata, and no stable page-specific canonical title/description in source HTML.
action: Generate one static HTML page per verified entity and retain the JSON-powered interface as progressive enhancement.

finding: Temple history values explicitly contain placeholder language.
risk: Placeholder statements may be exposed to users and indexed as production content.
action: Mark these records as draft, remove them from public indexation, and replace them only with source-backed summaries.

finding: Every JSON request appends ?v=44 and uses cache:no-store.
risk: The static site cannot benefit from normal browser/PWA caching, and the hard-coded version is stale after many batches.
action: Use a release version constant and normal cache semantics with service-worker-managed invalidation.

finding: Several tag arrays repeat the same slug twice.
risk: Search weighting and metadata quality are degraded.
action: Deduplicate and normalize tags during the search-index rebuild.

finding: Recent commits use the generic message 'Add files via upload'.
risk: It is difficult to trace which batch changed which production path or to roll back safely.
action: Use batch-specific commit messages and release tags from Batch 143 onward.
